379		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
380		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
381		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
382		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
383		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
384		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
385		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
386		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
387		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
388		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
389		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
390		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours

394	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
395	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
396	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
397	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
398	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
399		if configured == "" {
400			configured = DefaultLogDir
401		}
402		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
403			return configured
404		}
405		if projectRoot == "" {
406			return configured
407		}
408		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
409	}

39	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
40	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
41	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
42	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
43	// captured.
44	//
45	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
46	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
47	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
48		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
49	
50		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
51		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
52	
53		var agent *AgentConfig
54		var workers *WorkersConfig
55		if c != nil {
56			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
57			workers = c.Workers
58		}
59	
60		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
61		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
62		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
63	
64		r.harness = overrides.Harness
65		if r.harness == "" && agent != nil {
66			r.harness = agent.Harness
67		}
68	
69		r.model = overrides.Model
70		if r.model == "" && agent != nil {
71			r.model = agent.Model
72		}
73	
74		r.provider = overrides.Provider
75		r.modelRef = overrides.ModelRef
76		r.profile = overrides.Profile
77		r.minTier = overrides.MinTier
78		r.maxTier = overrides.MaxTier
79		r.effort = overrides.Effort
80		r.minPower = overrides.MinPower
81		r.maxPower = overrides.MaxPower
82		r.passthrough = AgentPassthrough{
83			Harness:  r.harness,
84			Provider: r.provider,
85			Model:    r.model,
86		}
87	
88		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
89		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
90			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
91		}
92	
93		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
94			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
95		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
96			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
97		}
98	
99		if overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout != nil {
100			r.providerRequestTimeout = *overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout
101		}
102	
103		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
104	
105		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
106		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
107			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
108		}
109	
110		if agent != nil {
111			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
112			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
113				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
114				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
115					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
116				}
117			}
118		}

120			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
121		}

123		return r

3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
34	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
40		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
43		if harness == "" {
44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}
46		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
47			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
48			r := NewRunner(cfg)
49			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
50			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
51		}
52		if svc == nil {
53			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
54			if err != nil {
55				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
56			}
57			svc = built
58		}
59		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
60	}
61	
62	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
63	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
64	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
65	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
66	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
67		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
68		if harness == "" {
69			harness = rcfg.Harness()
70		}
71		model := runtime.ModelOverride
72		if model == "" {
73			model = rcfg.Model()
74		}
75		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
76		if permissions == "" {
77			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
78		}
79		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
80		if sessionLogDir == "" {
81			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
82		}
83	
84		var opts RunArgs
85		opts.Context = ctx
86		opts.Harness = harness
87		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
88		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
89		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
90		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
91		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
92		opts.Model = model
93		opts.Provider = rcfg.Provider()
94		opts.ModelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
95		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
96		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
97		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
98		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
99		opts.Permissions = permissions
100		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
101		return opts
102	}

1	# Agents — Power, Persona Dispatch
2	
3	> **Debugging routing?** Routing lives entirely in ddx-agent. See `CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service` (in the agent repo, `docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`) for the public surface; routing internals are inside that module.
4	
5	DDx invokes AI coding agents through the upstream `ddx-agent` service. DDx
6	does not choose harnesses, providers, endpoints, or models on the normal work
7	path. It describes the work, requests an abstract power level, and lets the
8	agent route.
9	
10	## Power bounds
11	
12	Normal execution should use power bounds:
13	
14	```bash
15	ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
16	```
17	
18	Power is an integer scale exposed by the agent contract. DDx passes
19	`MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` bounds and treats them as ordering
20	constraints, not as model identities. The agent reports what it actually used:
21	
22	```text
23	running with qwen 3.6-27b (power 10)
24	```
25	
26	DDx owns bead retry policy. It decides whether an attempt succeeded using DDx
27	evidence: commits, no-changes rationale, merge/preserve result, gates, review
28	verdicts, and cooldowns. If a retry is allowed, DDx may raise `MinPower`.
29	
30	The agent owns route selection within those requested bounds. DDx may query the
31	agent's available model/power catalog to choose a `MinPower` threshold such as
32	"top models only", but it must not pin a concrete model/provider on the normal
33	work path.
34	
35	## Passthrough constraints
36	
37	`--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are allowed only as passthrough
38	constraints:
39	
40	```bash
41	ddx run --min-power 10 --model qwen3.6-27b --prompt task.md
42	```
43	
44	DDx sends these values to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate them,
45	score them, use them for queue policy, rewrite them on retry, or use them as a
46	fallback mechanism. If the values conflict with requested power bounds or
47	availability, the agent owns the typed error or actual route. DDx stops on
48	hard-pin exhaustion; it does not remove pins, widen pins, call `ResolveRoute`,
49	or retry in a loop.
50	
51	## Execution layering
52	
53	The intended command layering is:
54	
55	- `ddx run` — one agent `Execute` call with requested `MinPower`/`MaxPower`.
56	  No `ResolveRoute`.
57	- `ddx try <bead>` — one bead attempt layered over `run`.
58	- `ddx work` — queue drain and retry policy layered over `try`.
59	
60	`ResolveRoute` is status/debug-only. Do not use it in `run`, `try`, or `work`,
61	and never feed a `RouteDecision` back into `Execute`.
62	
63	## Personas
64	
65	Personas are reusable AI personality templates. DDx injects a
66	persona's body as a system-prompt addendum to `ddx run` —
67	same persona, same behavior across every harness, because the
68	harness sees a flat system prompt, not a persona file.
69	
70	### Default roster
71	
72	The default `ddx` plugin ships five role-focused personas:
73	
74	- `code-reviewer` — security-first review with structured verdicts
75	- `test-engineer` — stubs-over-mocks, real-e2e, baselined
76	  performance, testing pyramid as shape not ratios
77	- `implementer` — YAGNI / KISS / DOWITYTD, ships tests with code
78	- `architect` — opinionated on when to reach for each pattern
79	  (monolith-first, data-model-first)
80	- `specification-enforcer` — refuses drift from governing artifacts
81	
82	See `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md` for the quality bar and
83	authoring guidance. Projects can install additional personas via
84	plugins.
85	
86	### Using a persona
87	
88	Directly at invocation time:
89	
90	```bash
91	ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
92	```
93	
94	Or by binding a role in `.ddx/config.yaml`:
95	
96	```yaml
97	persona_bindings:
98	  code-reviewer: code-reviewer
99	  test-engineer: test-engineer
100	  architect: architect
101	```
102	
103	Workflows then reference the role abstractly (e.g., "dispatch to
104	the `code-reviewer` role"), and DDx resolves the binding at
105	dispatch time.
106	
107	```bash
108	ddx persona list                # available personas
109	ddx persona show <name>         # persona body + frontmatter
110	ddx persona bind <role> <name>  # set a binding
111	ddx persona bindings            # current role → persona map
112	```
113	
114	## Quorum
115	
116	For multi-agent review or adversarial checks, use a workflow skill composition
117	such as `compare-prompts`, covered in `reference/review.md`:
118	
119	```bash
120	ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
121	ddx run --persona architect --prompt review.md
122	```
123	
124	## Getting more capabilities
125	
126	`ddx install <name>` adds plugins to the project. Plugins can ship
127	personas, prompts, patterns, templates, and workflow skills.
128	
129	```bash
130	ddx install helix              # HELIX workflow plugin
131	ddx search <term>              # discover available plugins
132	ddx installed                  # list installed plugins
133	ddx uninstall <name>           # remove
134	```
135	
136	Custom personas go in `.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/personas/<name>.md`
137	(or directly in `library/personas/<name>.md` for local-only use).
138	See the personas README for the authoring quality bar.
139	
140	## Anti-patterns
141	
142	- **Stacking power bounds and `--model` / `--harness` casually**. Normal DDx
143	  work should request `MinPower`. Use passthrough pins only for explicit
144	  operator constraints; DDx must not interpret them.
145	- **Hardcoding a harness name in a workflow**. Workflow should
146	  dispatch by power or role binding; letting each project route through its
147	  agent configuration is the point of DDx's agent boundary.
148	- **Persona for everything**. Personas add a system-prompt addendum;
149	  they don't make a bad prompt good. Start with a clear prompt;
150	  reach for a persona when you want consistent style/standards
151	  across invocations.
152	- **Persona files in skill directories**. Personas live in
153	  `library/personas/` or `.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/personas/`, not in
154	  `.claude/skills/` or `.agents/skills/`. Don't mix the two.
155	
156	## CLI reference
157	
158	```bash
159	# Dispatch
160	ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
161	ddx run --top-power --prompt task.md
162	ddx run --min-power 10 --max-power 20 --prompt task.md
163	ddx run --min-power 10 --provider openrouter --model qwen3.6-27b --prompt task.md
164	ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
165	
166	# Introspection
167	ddx agent list                         # available harnesses
168	ddx agent capabilities                 # model + power options
169	ddx agent doctor                       # harness health
170	ddx agent log                          # recent invocation metadata
171	ddx agent log <session-id>             # one invocation's detail
172	
173	# Personas
174	ddx persona list
175	ddx persona show <name>
176	ddx persona bind <role> <persona>
177	ddx persona bindings
178	
179	# Plugin install
180	ddx install <plugin>
181	ddx search <term>
182	ddx installed
183	ddx uninstall <name>
184	```
185	
186	Full flag list: `ddx run --help`, `ddx persona --help`,
187	`ddx install --help`.
188

> `ResolveRoute` is status/debug-only. Do not use it in `run`, `try`, or `work`, and never feed a `RouteDecision` back into `Execute`.

526	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentCondenseCommand() *cobra.Command {
527		cmd := &cobra.Command{
528			Use:   "condense",
529			Short: "Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines",
530			Long: `Read raw agent output from stdin and write condensed output to stdout.
531	
532	Keeps: namespace-prefixed progress lines, tool calls, errors/warnings, issue IDs,
533	markdown structure (#, |, **), and phase markers. Drops raw diffs, codex
534	boilerplate (Commands run:, tokens used), and bulk verbose content.`,
535			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
536				namespace, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("namespace")
537				data, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
538				if err != nil {
539					return fmt.Errorf("reading stdin: %w", err)
540				}
541				result := agent.CondenseOutput(string(data), namespace)
542				if result != "" {
543					fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result)
544					if !strings.HasSuffix(result, "\n") {
545						fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout())
546					}
547				}
548				return nil
549			},
550		}
551		cmd.Flags().String("namespace", "helix:", "Caller namespace prefix to keep (e.g. helix:)")
552		return cmd
553	}
554

14	// agentTestDir creates a temp dir with a minimal DDx config (no harness set, so
15	// routing picks from all registered candidates).
16	// HOME is redirected to a clean temp dir to prevent the global ~/.config/agent/config.yaml
17	// from being loaded (which would cause tests to attempt real network connections).
18	func agentTestDir(t *testing.T) string {
19		t.Helper()
20		dir := t.TempDir()
21		t.Setenv("HOME", dir)
22		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
23		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
24		cfg := `version: "1.0"
25	library:
26	  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
27	  repository:
28	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
29	    branch: "main"
30	`
31		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfg), 0o644))
32		return dir
33	}

46	// TestAgentRunProfileFlagWithVirtualHarness verifies that --profile is accepted as a
47	// valid flag and does not interfere with an explicit --harness virtual invocation.
48	func TestAgentRunProfileFlagWithVirtualHarness(t *testing.T) {
49		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
50		// Inject a virtual response for "hello"
51		t.Setenv("DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES", `[{"prompt_match":"hello","response":"hi from virtual"}]`)
52	
53		dir := agentTestDirWithHarness(t, "virtual")
54		rootCmd := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
55		output, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "agent", "run",
56			"--harness", "virtual",
57			"--profile", "cheap",

59		)

61		assert.Contains(t, output, "hi from virtual")
62	}

64	// TestAgentRunProfileFlagRecognized verifies that --profile is a recognized flag
65	// (flag parsing does not fail with "unknown flag: --profile").
66	func TestAgentRunProfileFlagRecognized(t *testing.T) {
67		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
68	
69		dir := agentTestDir(t)
70		rootCmd := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
71	
72		// The run will fail (agent provider not configured), but flag parsing must succeed.
73		// A "unknown flag" error means the flag was not registered.
74		// --timeout 3s prevents exponential-backoff retries from hanging the test.
75		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "agent", "run", "--profile", "cheap", "--text", "test", "--timeout", "3s")
76		if err != nil {
77			assert.NotContains(t, err.Error(), "unknown flag", "--profile must be a registered flag")
78			assert.NotContains(t, err.Error(), "no harness available", "routing should find agent (always available)")
79		}
80	}

82	// TestAgentRunProfileRoutingSelectsViableHarness verifies that --profile cheap routes
83	// to a viable harness (not a routing failure) when no --harness is specified.
84	// The agent harness is always available (embedded), so cheap routing picks it.
85	// We confirm routing succeeded by checking the error is from agent internals, not routing.
86	func TestAgentRunProfileRoutingSelectsViableHarness(t *testing.T) {
87		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
88	
89		dir := agentTestDir(t)
90		rootCmd := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
91	
92		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "agent", "run", "--profile", "cheap", "--text", "test", "--timeout", "3s")
93		// Expect either success or an agent-provider error (not a routing or flag error).
94		if err != nil {
95			msg := err.Error()
96			assert.NotContains(t, msg, "unknown flag", "flag must be recognized")
97			assert.NotContains(t, msg, "unknown harness", "routing must resolve a harness")
98			assert.NotContains(t, msg, "no harness available", "a viable harness must exist")
99			// Error should be from agent runtime, not from routing layer.
100			isAgentErr := strings.Contains(msg, "agent:") ||
101				strings.Contains(msg, "provider") ||
102				strings.Contains(msg, "timeout") ||
103				strings.Contains(msg, "exited with code")
104			assert.True(t, isAgentErr, "error should be from agent runtime after routing, got: %s", msg)
105		}
106	}

577	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the thin worker: it creates an isolated worktree,
578	// constructs the agent prompt from bead context, runs the agent harness,
579	// synthesizes a commit if the agent left uncommitted changes, then cleans up
580	// the worktree and returns the result. It classifies outcomes as exactly one
581	// of:
582	//
583	//   - task_succeeded: agent exited 0 and produced one or more commits
584	//   - task_failed:    agent exited non-zero
585	//   - task_no_changes: agent exited 0, made no commits, and wrote a rationale
586	//   - task_no_evidence: agent exited 0 but made no commits and wrote no rationale
587	//
588	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, preserve-ref management, and orphan
589	// recovery are the parent's responsibility (see LandBeadResult, RecoverOrphans).
590	//
591	// Agent dispatch: production callers leave runtime.Service and runtime.AgentRunner
592	// nil. ExecuteBeadWithConfig constructs a fresh agentlib.DdxAgent from
593	// projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir and dispatches via RunViaServiceWith.
594	// Tests may set runtime.AgentRunner to inject a fake that returns canned
595	// Result values; when set, it takes precedence over the service path.
596	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
597		if ctx == nil {
598			ctx = context.Background()
599		}
600		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
601		if runtime.WorkerID == "" {
602			runtime.WorkerID = os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID")
603		}
604	
605		wtPath := executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID)
606		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(wtPath), 0o755); mkErr != nil {
607			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating execute-bead worktree parent dir: %w", mkErr)
608		}
609	
610		// Commit beads.jsonl before spawning worktree so the worktree snapshot
611		// includes any bead metadata updates (e.g. spec-id).
612		if err := CommitTracker(projectRoot); err != nil {
613			return nil, err
614		}
615	
616		// Checkpoint any remaining caller dirt as a real commit on the current
617		// branch (FEAT-012 §22, US-126 AC#1). The new HEAD becomes the effective
618		// base for the worker worktree; caller's edits are preserved as a normal
619		// commit they can `git reset HEAD~` if they want them back uncommitted.
620		if _, err := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(projectRoot, "chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead "+attemptID); err != nil {
621			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-execute-bead checkpoint: %w", err)
622		}
623	
624		// Resolve base revision after the tracker + checkpoint commits.
625		baseRev, err := resolveBase(gitOps, projectRoot, runtime.FromRev)
626		if err != nil {
627			return nil, err
628		}
629	
630		// Create the isolated worktree. Orphan recovery is the parent's responsibility
631		// (call RecoverOrphans before invoking workers).
632		if err := gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wtPath, baseRev); err != nil {
633			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating isolated worktree: %w", err)
634		}
